summary:
Clindamycin or metronidazole can be used to treat bacterial vaginosis during pregnancy due to their effectiveness and safety profiles; these recommendations align with current medical guidelines aimed at ensuring maternal health without compromising fetal well-being. Agent1 reasoned by referencing established treatment options and confirming they were considered safe for use during pregnancy per available literature. Key takeaways include efficacy of either drug choice along with assurance regarding safety throughout gestation period.
